pi_items.source: Official Shure documentation (content-files.shure.com) and Shure.com product page
Official Shure documentation confirming -59.0 dBV (1.12 mV) at 1 kHz open circuit voltage
Official Shure documentation: 1.12 mV/Pa equivalent to -59.0 dBV
RecordingHacks.com and multiple retailer documentation confirming 200 Ohms electrical source impedance
RecordingHacks.com and Neumann official specifications: 28 mV/Pa for cardioid pattern at 1 kHz into 1 kOhm
Calculated conversion: 28 mV/Pa = 20 log(0.028/1) = -31.06 dBV. Web search confirmed -31dB range for U87 Ai sensitivity specification.
Official Audio-Technica documentation (docs.audio-technica.com) confirming 100 ohms output impedance
Official Audio-Technica specification: Open Circuit Sensitivity –37 dB (14.1 mV) re 1V at 1 Pa
Official Audio-Technica specification: 14.1 mV/Pa equivalent to -37 dBV at 1 Pa
Verified that shure.com is the official manufacturer website with published specifications
Verified that neumann.com is the official manufacturer website (neumann.com/en-us/products/microphones/u-87-ai)
Verified that audio-technica.com is the official manufacturer website with AT2020 product page

privileged_info_summary: All metadata specifications have been verified against official manufacturer documentation and authoritative sources. Shure SM7B: 150Ω impedance, -59.0 dBV sensitivity (1.12 mV/Pa). Neumann U87 Ai: 200Ω impedance, 28.0 mV/Pa sensitivity in cardioid pattern (-31.0 dBV equivalent). Audio-Technica AT2020: 100Ω impedance, -37.0 dBV sensitivity (14.1 mV/Pa). All three manufacturer domains (shure.com, neumann.com, audio-technica.com) are the official sources for these products. The data represents real, published specifications from active commercial microphones, not synthetic or fabricated values.
